message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: The Original Las Casuelas (Palm Springs)
Cuisine/type signal: Mexican (Mazatlán-style, family-owned)
Why it surfaced in discovery: Opened 1958 by Maria and Florencio 'Del' Delgado; oldest continuously-operating restaurant in Palm Springs and progenitor of the multi-generation Las Casuelas family (Terraza, Nuevas, Cafe) across the Valley.
Discovery sources: https://www.lascasuelasoriginal.com/story | https://www.lascasuelasnuevas.com/our-history | https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/3-longest-operating-restaurants-in-palm-springs/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

Here are the search results for **Original Las Casuelas** in Palm Springs, including reviews from Yelp and Google:

## Restaurant Overview

The Original Las Casuelas is a timeless landmark in Palm Springs, CA, that has been serving authentic and traditional Mexican cuisine since 1958, when it was founded by Maria and Florencio "Del" Delgado. The restaurant specializes in homemade Sonoran-style cuisine.

## Location & Hours
The restaurant is located at 368 N Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA 92262, and is open Mon-Thu 11am-9pm, Fri-Sat 11am-10pm, and Sun 11am-9pm.

## Reviews Summary

The reviews are mixed:

**Positive Reviews:**
- Reviewers praise the grilled fish tacos, fajitas, smothered burritos, shrimp quesadilla, and shrimp fajitas
- Visitors appreciate the affordability compared to other restaurants in the area and enjoy the complimentary chips and salsa
- Some diners noted the service was exceptional, with staff being courteous, kind, expedient and professional

**Negative Reviews:**
- Some reviewers reported horrible service and waited 10-15 minutes before being served
- The service can be slow sometimes according to some reviews

**Overall Sentiment:**
Las Casuelas Original, a staple Mexican restaurant in Palm Springs since 1958, offers generous portions of delicious food with quick and friendly service according to many reviewers.

**Founding Year:** 1958

**Founders:** Maria and Florencio "Del" Delgado

**Current Ownership:** Florence Delgado (Owner/Operator) and Alana Coffin (Owner/Operator)

**History:** The restaurant has operated for over 65 years as an authentic Mexican cuisine establishment in the Coachella Valley. The founding family has maintained multi-generational stewardship of the business.

**Key Staff:**
- Pablo Gutiérrez - Manager (40+ years tenure)

**Location:** 368 N Palm Canyon, Palm Springs, CA

**Notable Recognition:**
- Featured in Palm Springs Life magazine
- Reviewed in The Desert Sun

**Cuisine Focus:** Authentic Mexican cuisine with "timeless recipes" passed down through generations

# Original Las Casuelas - Palm Springs Review Summary

**Rating & Reviews:**
- 4.0 of 5 bubbles
- 376 total reviews

**Awards:**

- Compliments for guacamole, chips, and margaritas

*Negative:*

- Food quality concerns: bland vegetarian options, greasy ground beef

**Overall Pattern:** Mixed experiences with deteriorating quality concerns. Long-time patrons express disappointment with recent visits compared to the restaurant's historical reputation after approximately 50 years in operation.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best Mexican restaurants in the Palm Springs and Coachella Valley area for 2025:

## Top Recommendations

Clandestino was named more frequently than any other Mexican restaurant by local experts, operated by the same couple that runs Tac/Quila, with a menu featuring elevated plates in a more relaxed, open-air space with views of the San Jacinto mountains.

Tac/Quila is a celebrated downtown hotspot that gets high marks from foodies for bold flavors and thoughtful presentation, with a menu inspired by Mexico's Jalisco region including elevated classics like street tacos with lobster and seared ahi.

El Mexicali Café opened its doors 30 years ago and has become one of the most renowned restaurants in Greater Palm Springs and a locals' favorite.

## Other Notable Options

El Mirasol serves its winning Mexican cuisine at two Palm Springs locations, including one located in Los Arboles Hotel offering authentic Mexican cuisine crafted from fresh ingredients and traditional Castaneda family recipes.

Jalisco in Coachella is family owned and operated since 1980 and has nearly tripled its capacity since opening, with locals labeling their birria soup and tortas as "must try".

Maracas is a downtown Palm Springs staple with a festive vibe, solid drinks, and classic Mexican fare.

Maleza inside the Drift Hotel receives positive reviews for its delicious takes on Baja-inspired fusion cuisine, inventive cocktails and mocktails, and excellent service.

input.query: "Las Casuelas Original" Palm Springs "1958" oldest restaurant Delgado history

input.prompt: What does this say about Las Casuelas Original and its longevity?

content.content:
# Las Casuelas Original: A Palm Springs Institution

According to the webpage, Las Casuelas Original has demonstrated remarkable staying power in Palm Springs's dining scene. The restaurant is "widely recognized as the first Mexican restaurant in Palm Springs and one of its longest-operating dining institutions," having served customers since 1958.

The establishment's longevity reflects its deep roots in the community. Founded by the Delgado family, it "remains a cornerstone of downtown dining" and has successfully maintained its presence for over six decades. This enduring legacy speaks to both the quality of its authentic Mexican cuisine and its cultural significance to the city's culinary identity.

Web search results for query: ""Las Casuelas Original" Palm Springs "1958" oldest restaurant Delgado history"

Links: [{"title":"Story | Experience Palm Springs History — Las Casuelas Original Restaurant","url":"https://www.lascasuelasoriginal.com/story"},{"title":"About us | Las Casuelas Terraza","url":"https://www.lascasuelas.com/about-us"},{"title":"Las Casuelas Original | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/las-casuelas-original-palm-springs/"},{"title":"THE ORIGINAL LAS CASUELAS - Updated April 2026 - 527 Photos & 635 Reviews - 368 N Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, California - Mexican - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Menu - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/the-original-las-casuelas-palm-springs-2"},{"title":"Home | Las Casuelas Terraza","url":"https://www.lascasuelas.com/"},{"title":"Our History | Mexican Food | California | Las Casuelas Nuevas","url":"https://www.lascasuelasnuevas.com/our-history"},{"title":"Las Casuelas - The Original","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/eat-and-drink/las-casuelas-the-original/"},{"title":"Las Casuelas Terraza | Historic Mexican Restaurant in Downtown Palm Springs","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/a-little-slice-of-mexico-at-las-casuelas-terraza/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Las Casuelas Original and its history:

## Restaurant Founding

Las Casuelas Original was founded in February 1958 when Maria and Florencio "Del" Delgado brought their timeless recipes to Palm Springs. For more than 65 years, The Original Las Casuelas has been a beloved part of the Coachella Valley.

## Family History and Origins

The Delgado family's culinary tradition goes back much further than 1958. The family legacy traces back to Maria Fajardo, the Delgado family matriarch, who cooked for copper miners in pre-depression Arizona with her famous earthenware pots and pans, cazuelas. The tradition began in the 1900s in the culturally rich city of Mazatlán, Mexico, where founder Florencio "Del" Delgado's grandmother prepared sumptuous dishes and perfected classic Mexican recipes.

## Notable Details

An interesting piece of history: The spelling of Casuelas was a fortunate marketing typo during a cash strapped opening that has stuck with the restaurants and has become proprietary for the family's hospitality and recipes.

Over the years, other Delgado family members opened Las Casuelas restaurants around the Coachella Valley, including Las Casuelas Nuevas in Rancho Mirage, Casuelas Café in Palm Desert, and Las Casuelas Terraza in downtown Palm Springs.

Links: [{"title":"Historic Palm Springs Restaurants: Iconic Dining & Local ...","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/3-longest-operating-restaurants-in-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Discover Historic Dining in Palm Springs","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/3-longest-operating-restaurants-in-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Las Casuelas Terraza | Historic Mexican Restaurant in Downtown Palm Springs","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/a-little-slice-of-mexico-at-las-casuelas-terraza/"},{"title":"Oldest Restaurants in the Desert Bank on Good Food, Service, Ambiance","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/the-oldest-restaurants-in-the-desert/"},{"title":"8 Historic Restaurants in Greater Palm Springs That Have Stood the Test of Time","url":"https://localemagazine.com/best-historic-restaurants-in-greater-palm-springs/"},{"title":"THE ORIGINAL LAS CASUELAS - Updated April 2026 - 527 Photos & 635 Reviews - 368 N Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, California - Mexican - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Menu - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/the-original-las-casuelas-palm-springs-2"},{"title":"Great old school Mexican - Review of Original Las Casuelas, Palm Springs, CA - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/ShowUserReviews-g32847-d486267-r129139151-Original_Las_Casuelas-Palm_Springs_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"About us | Las Casuelas Terraza","url":"https://www.lascasuelas.com/about-us"},{"title":"Posts in oldest restaurants in palm springs | Blog","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/all/tag/oldest-restaurants-in-palm-springs/6732a2f349ec280a092a2462/"},{"title":"Historic Restaurants in Greater Palm Springs","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/blog/post/historic-restaurants-in-greater-palm-springs/"}]

Based on the search results, I've found information about the historic Mexican restaurant in Palm Springs:

## The Original Las Casuelas

The Original Las Casuelas was the first Mexican restaurant in Palm Springs and the oldest restaurant in Palm Springs. It has been serving iconic Mexican food since 1958.

The restaurant was opened by Maria and Florencio "Del" Delgado in 1958 and continues to satisfy customers with scratch-made family recipes highlighting the flavors of Mazatán, Mexico.

### Interesting Details

The restaurant is named after an earthenware cooking pot called a "cazuela," though the name is misspelled on the sign due to a marketing company error, and the family opted to keep the misspelling since replacing it would be costly.

The restaurant has a unique family heritage: The legacy traces back to Maria Fajardo, the Delgado family matriarch, who cooked for copper miners in pre-depression Arizona with her famous earthenware pots and pans, cazuelas.

The Original Las Casuelas is described as a timeless landmark in Palm Springs serving authentic and traditional Mexican cuisine.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

input.name: The Original Las Casuelas
input.corridor: downtown Palm Springs (N Palm Canyon Dr, uptown-adjacent)

reputation.sentiment: mixed (positive on heritage/value/portions; recurring complaints on service inconsistency and 'used to be better')
reputation.recency_signal: Recent reviews praise affordability, complimentary chips/salsa, grilled fish tacos, fajitas, and friendly long-tenured staff, but also flag slow service, forgotten orders, cleanliness issues, and a sense the restaurant has slipped from its historical peak
reputation.longevity: Operating since February 1958 (~68 years); continuous Delgado-family ownership, currently operated by Florence Delgado and Alana Coffin; manager Pablo Gutiérrez 40+ years, head chef Mario Villareal 25+ years
credentials: Featured by Palm Springs Life (restaurant directory entry)
Listed by Visit Palm Springs / Visit Greater Palm Springs as a historic/longest-operating restaurant
Cited as the first Mexican restaurant and oldest continuously-operating restaurant in Palm Springs

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces strongly for 'oldest/historic Palm Springs restaurant' and 'Las Casuelas history' queries. Does NOT surface in current 'best Mexican restaurant Palm Springs / Coachella Valley 2025' agent results — those are dominated by Clandestino, Tac/Quila, El Mirasol, El Mexicali Café, Maleza, and Maracas. Also absent from 'date night Palm Springs' and 'fine dining' lists.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: When it does surface, the description is accurate (1958 founding, Delgado family, Mazatlán heritage, oldest in Palm Springs). The 'Sonoran-style' framing in some agent answers conflicts with the family's own Mazatlán positioning — minor description drift.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: lascasuelasoriginal.com has clean navigation (Home, Gallery, Menu, Story, Reservations, FAQ), legible hours (Sun-Thu 11-9, Fri-Sat 11-10), address and phone in plain text, and Yelp/Instagram links. No evident schema.org Restaurant markup, no machine-readable menu structure, no LocalBusiness JSON-LD detected — site is human-readable but agent-thin.

competitors: Las Casuelas Terraza (sibling, downtown Palm Springs — 3,263 Yelp reviews, far higher volume)
Maracas Cantina (downtown Palm Springs, festive Mexican)
relationships.corridor_role: supporting heritage anchor — N Palm Canyon Dr uptown-adjacent. It is the historical progenitor of the Las Casuelas family but is overshadowed in current downtown PSP dining conversation by sibling Terraza (S Palm Canyon) and newer entrants like Tac/Quila and Clandestino.
query_wins: Las Casuelas history / Delgado family origins

convertibility.ownership_signal: independent (Delgado family, multi-generational; currently Florence Delgado and Alana Coffin as owner/operators with a direct email alana@lascasuelasoriginal.com)
convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Owns a real branded site (lascasuelasoriginal.com) with reservations and FAQ pages, maintains Yelp and Instagram, appears in Palm Springs Life directory and Visit Palm Springs / Visit Greater Palm Springs listings. No visible agency footprint or paid premium listings; structured data is absent — suggests light, in-house marketing rather than retained agency.
convertibility.reasoning: This is a classic convertible target: independent family ownership with a reachable named operator (Alana Coffin, public email), a functional but agent-thin website, a strong heritage moat (oldest in Palm Springs since 1958) that agents are NOT surfacing in current 'best Mexican' queries, and clear marketing investment (real domain, reservation flow, social). The gap between reputation (Travelers' Choice, 68-year longevity, founding family of a four-restaurant Valley dynasty) and agent visibility (absent from 2025 best-of lists dominated by newer concepts) is exactly the wedge an AICV pitch fits. Risk: declining recent-review sentiment could mean some of the visibility gap is earned rather than purely a discoverability problem.

flags: Google review count and star rating [UNVERIFIED] — Tripadvisor 403'd on direct fetch but search snippet confirmed 376 reviews / 4.0 stars and Travelers' Choice; Yelp 635 reviews confirmed via search snippet but star rating [UNVERIFIED]
Palm Springs Life article fetch returned 403 — directory listing exists but full editorial content [UNVERIFIED]
Claim that residents have voted it 'Best Mexican Restaurant year after year' appeared in a search snippet but the specific publication, year, and award are [UNVERIFIED] — not asserted in credentials
No James Beard, Michelin, Food Network, or Yelp Top 100 recognition found — credentials list is intentionally limited to what was verified
schema.org / JSON-LD presence assessed via rendered-page inspection only; raw HTML not directly inspected [UNVERIFIED whether any embedded structured data exists in source]
Sentiment characterization 'mixed' is based on Tripadvisor and Yelp snippet patterns; a full Google Reviews recency pass was not completed
